rulings.Case Title: JIM KELLY et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Requiring Defendant John K. Bice Company Inc. To Verify Its Written Discovery Responses And Deeming Plaintiffs Requests For Admission Admitted; And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, August 29, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiffs¿ motion for an order requiring defendant John K. Bice Comapny Inc. to verify its responses to written discovery and deeming plaintiffs¿ requests for admission admitted is denied. Defendant, a dissolved corporation, is under no obligation to provide verifications. (Maldonado v. Superior Court (2002) 94 Cal.App.4th 1390.) Since defendant is not required to provide verifications, defendant¿s timely answers to plaintiffs¿ requests for admission will not be deemed admitted. Both plaintiffs¿ and defendant¿s requests for monetary sanctions are denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am, not 9:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: IRVING JUAREZ et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Defendant Lg E And Ku Capital Llc To Produce Custodian(S) Of Records And Person(S) Most Knowledgeable For Deposition And Responsive Documents, And Plaintiffs¿ Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, August 29, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel LG&E and Ku Capital LLC to produce custodian(s) of records and person(s) most knowledgeable for deposition and responsive documents and request for sanctions of $1061.00 is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am, not 9:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
